question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier's lack of fee-field immutability enforcement to modify the StreamFile's royalty fee numerator/denominator after verified() is called but before the parser commits the data, causing incorrect royalty fee amounts to be stored?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeStreamFileNotifier iterating over a mutable notifiers list to inject a custom StreamFileNotifier implementation at runtime that intercepts and modifies fee fields in the StreamFile before passing it to the legitimate downstream notifier?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier's fixed thread pool of size 2 by exhausting both executor threads with long-running parse operations, causing the queue to fill and new fee-bearing StreamFiles passed to verified() to block indefinitely, effectively creating a fee processing denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verified() does not validate that the StreamFile's fee-exempt key list is consistent with the on-chain state, allowing a crafted StreamFile to mark additional accounts as fee-exempt and cause the mirror node to report incorrect fee exemption status?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier interface's single-method contract to pass a StreamFile whose getConsensusStart() returns a timestamp far in the future, causing the Downloader's lastStreamFile cache to be updated with a future-dated fee record and blocking all subsequent legitimate fee file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of fee-collector account validation in verified() to pass a StreamFile where custom fee collector account IDs have been replaced with attacker-controlled account IDs, causing the mirror node to report fees as being collected by the wrong accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier's StreamFileSubscriber.run() loop catching all exceptions and logging them without re-throwing, allowing a fee-manipulated StreamFile to cause a parse error that is silently swallowed, leaving the fee record unprocessed without any alert?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier.verified() interface's lack of a return value or acknowledgment mechanism to cause a scenario where the notifier silently drops a fee-bearing StreamFile (e.g., due to queue overflow) without the caller being aware, resulting in missing fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verified() in CutoverServiceImpl only processes RecordFile instances and silently ignores other StreamFile types, to pass a non-RecordFile StreamFile containing fee data that bypasses the lastRecordFile update, causing fee state desynchronization during cutover?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier interface's acceptance of null-annotated StreamFile fields (despite @NullMarked) to pass a StreamFile with null fee-related fields that bypass downstream null checks, causing NullPointerExceptions that disrupt fee processing and leave the database in an incomplete state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier's flush logic based on exceedsInterval() to manipulate system clock signals and force a flush of a partially-assembled fee batch, causing the parser to process an incomplete set of fee records and store incorrect aggregate fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeStreamFileNotifier's lack of ordering guarantees among notifiers to cause a fee-bearing StreamFile to be processed by the CutoverServiceImpl notifier before the BatchStreamFileNotifier, resulting in the cutover state being updated with fee data that has not yet been persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier interface's lack of fee-schedule-file cross-reference validation to pass a RecordFile whose transactions reference a fee schedule (file 0.0.111) that has been tampered with in the stream, causing the mirror node to apply incorrect fee rates?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier's queue.put() blocking behavior to cause a deadlock between the downloader thread calling verified() and the subscriber thread processing fees, resulting in a permanent halt of fee record ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum fee value check in verified() to inject a StreamFile with a max_fee field set to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing downstream fee comparison logic to always evaluate the transaction as within fee limits and bypassing fee cap enforcement in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier interface's lack of StreamFile integrity re-check after the object is enqueued in BatchStreamFileNotifier to modify the StreamFile's fee items between enqueue and dequeue, causing the parser to process attacker-modified fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verified() does not validate the StreamFile's network/shard/realm fields against the expected mirror node configuration, allowing a StreamFile from a different network with different fee parameters to be ingested and corrupt the fee database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier's lack of fee-type consistency validation to pass a StreamFile where FIXED_FEE entries have been replaced with FRACTIONAL_FEE entries post-verification, causing the mirror node to store incorrect fee type classifications that affect fee reporting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier's buffer.add() without size limit (beyond queue capacity) to cause an OutOfMemoryError during fee batch processing, resulting in the JVM crashing and all buffered fee records being lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier interface's single verified() method to pass a StreamFile with an empty items list but non-zero fee totals in metadata fields, causing the mirror node to record fee revenue without corresponding transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of fee-denominating-token validation in verified() to pass a StreamFile where custom fees denominated in a non-existent token ID are included, causing the mirror node to store fee records referencing invalid token entities?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeStreamFileNotifier's verified() method iterating over all notifiers without catching exceptions between iterations, so that a RuntimeException thrown by one notifier prevents subsequent notifiers from receiving the fee-bearing StreamFile, causing partial fee persistence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier interface's lack of fee-schedule-key validation to pass a StreamFile where the feeScheduleKey field has been replaced with an attacker-controlled key, causing the mirror node to store an incorrect fee schedule key that could be used to authorize future fee modifications?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier's StreamFileSubscriber handling of null streamFile (from queue.poll timeout) to trigger a spurious flush of buffered fee records when the buffer is non-empty and the interval is exceeded, causing fee records to be processed in an unintended batch composition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileNotifier interface's lack of consensus-timestamp ordering enforcement to pass StreamFiles with out-of-order timestamps to verified(), causing the fee processing pipeline to store fees with incorrect temporal ordering and breaking fee history queries?
